findings.title: Over-broad capability claims in description
findings.description: The skill description claims it can 'Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality' and 'Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.' While this is a frontend design skill, the description is somewhat broad and could be interpreted as promising capabilities beyond typical frontend generation, such as full application architecture or backend integration. However, this is a minor concern as the skill's scope is clearly frontend-focused.
findings.evidence: Description: 'Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.'
findings.remediation: Consider narrowing the description to more precisely match the skill's actual capabilities, e.g., 'Generate frontend code for web components, pages, or applications with a focus on distinctive aesthetics.'

findings.title: Missing allowed-tools specification
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest does not include an 'allowed-tools' field. While this field is optional per the agent skills specification, its absence means there is no explicit declaration of which agent tools (Read, Write, Bash, Python, etc.) this skill is permitted to use. This is a minor documentation issue that could be improved for clarity and security.
findings.evidence: YAML frontmatter: name, description, license, compatibility fields present but no allowed-tools field.
findings.remediation: Add an 'allowed-tools' field to the YAML frontmatter specifying which agent tools this skill requires, e.g., 'allowed-tools: [Read, Write]' if it only needs to read user input and write code output.

findings.title: License reference to non-existent file
findings.description: The license field in the YAML frontmatter states 'Complete terms in LICENSE.txt', but no LICENSE.txt file was provided in the skill package. This could be a minor documentation oversight or potentially misleading if the license terms are important for compliance.
findings.evidence: license: Complete terms in LICENSE.txt - but no LICENSE.txt file is present in the skill directory.
findings.remediation: Either include a LICENSE.txt file with the actual license terms, or update the license field to a standard license identifier (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0) and remove the reference to the missing file.
